text.title: `agent-fixable` Triage Gate (Humans Pick, Agents Work) Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Triage bot has Edit/Write tools 'to also fix typos in the issue body' — same identity that applies the label.
antipattern.root-cause: Author conflated triage and coding identities.
antipattern.fix: Split into two service accounts: triage-bot-ro (read + label only) and agent-* (code-write, no label-write).

antipattern.symptom: Humans apply both `agent-fixable` AND `agent:devin` 'to save the router a step'. Tickets stick on Devin queue while Copilot is idle.
antipattern.root-cause: Vendor pinning at triage time, before the router sees fleet availability.
antipattern.fix: Forbid vendor labels at triage; let the router add them post-pickup based on current fleet load.

antipattern.symptom: On `no progress` the coding agent re-adds `agent-fixable` to its own ticket and busy-loops.
antipattern.fix: Strip label-write from the agent tool whitelist and enforce via repo policy.
